title: The hand-on-heart money breath
subtitle: A five-part ritual built around five rounds of paced breath, hand on the centre of the chest.
excerpt: A five-part Prepare / Release / Allow / Integrate / Anchor ritual built around five rounds of slow paced breath, hand on the centre of the chest. For the daytime moment the chest tightens and money is the first thing on the mind.
sourceNotes: The five-part Prepare / Release / Allow / Integrate / Anchor ritual structure is Rebecca J Page's framework, drawn from the twelve weekly ceremonies in The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The hand-on-heart anchor is public-domain somatic practice with a long lineage across body-based mindfulness traditions, popularised in modern form through Kristin Neff's self-compassion work (2003 onwards). The five-round paced-breath count, as a daytime money reset, is original to the homemade.education library.

practice.whenToUse: Use as a daytime reset when the chest tightens and money is the first thing on the mind: after a bill arrives, before a money conversation, in the gap between meetings.

content.text: A five-part ritual built around five rounds of slow paced breath, hand on the centre of the chest. For the daytime moment the chest tightens and money is the first thing on the mind. Five minutes; the structure does most of the work.
A quiet seat for five minutes. A timer set for five minutes if a closed end helps. Nothing else.

Sit somewhere you can stay for five minutes. Phone down. Feet flat on the floor. Place your right palm flat on the centre of the chest, the heel of the hand on the breastbone. Feel the warmth of the palm. Notice the chest moving under the palm with each breath in and out.

attrs.quote: I am ready to release the money grip the body has been holding today. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow steady ease with money in this body, today. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

content.text: Stay with the hand on the chest. Slow the breath. Five rounds, counted: in for four, out for six. The palm stays where it is, warm against the breastbone. The chest under the palm slows.
Notice the slowing without trying to push it. Five rounds is enough. If the chest has not slowed by the fifth round, five more rounds is fine; ten is the maximum for this ritual.
Lower the hand. Open the eyes if they are closed. Sit for one more breath. The ritual is closed.

The five-part Prepare / Release / Allow / Integrate / Anchor ritual structure is Rebecca J Page's framework, drawn from the twelve weekly ceremonies in The Money Journal (2025). The hand-on-heart anchor is public-domain somatic practice with a long lineage across body-based mindfulness traditions, popularised in modern form through Kristin Neff's self-compassion work (2003 onwards). The five-round paced-breath count, as a daytime money reset, is original to the homemade.education library.
